title: HTTP/3, QUIC, QPACK, Migration, and Production Protocol Defense · Execution Trace: H3 Control Request Streams
summary: A release claims HTTP/3 because UDP is open, ignores QUIC connection identity and migration, treats QPACK like HPACK, advertises Alt-Svc without rollback, and cannot defend parser, TLS, proxy, or capacity evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the h3 control request streams increment in multi-version protocol release and incident defense. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace HTTP/3 control streams, SETTINGS, request streams, frame placement, cancellation, GOAWAY, and connection versus stream errors.
Distinguish QUIC connection IDs, encrypted packets, transport parameters, bidirectional and unidirectional streams, migration, loss recovery, and UDP path behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict H3 Control Request Streams · Theory H3 Control Request Streams
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 1192 in a municipal gateway receiving one-byte TCP fragments. Build deterministic Go evidence to stage HTTP/3 through bounded QPACK, fallback health, and explicit rollback evidence; preserve this operating constraint: one parser state transition may consume bytes only after its full precondition holds; then lower one byte or time budget and prove rejection occurs before allocation or dispatch. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace HTTP/3 control streams, SETTINGS, request streams, frame placement, cancellation, GOAWAY, and connection versus stream errors.
